claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 16 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To help you check your eligibility, I need to start by asking about the child or children you're applying for.

**First, let me ask about the child's age:**

Could you tell me:
1. **How old is the child** you're claiming for?
2. **What is their name** (so I can keep track if you have multiple children)?

This will help me determine which eligibility rules apply to your situation. The age categories that matter are:
- Under 16 years old
- Between 16-19 years old
- 20 or over

Please share these details and we'll continue from there!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm inquiring about my two children actually.

My eldest is Alex, who's 18 years old. And then I have Blake, who's 13.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you check eligibility for both Alex (18 years old) and Blake (13 years old).

Let me work through each child's situation:

**Starting with Blake (13 years old):**

Since Blake is under 16, they automatically meet the age qualification. However, I need to check one important requirement:

**Is Blake working full-time?** (Full-time work means 24 hours or more per week on average)

This is important because children under 16 who work full-time are not eligible for Child Benefit.

---

Once we've sorted out Blake's situation, I'll then ask you some questions about Alex (18 years old), as the rules are different for 16-19 year olds - they need to be in approved education or training to qualify.

Let's start with Blake - are they working full-time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake doesn't work at all - they're only 13, so they're just in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Since Blake is 13 years old and not working full-time, they meet the basic qualifying child criteria.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now continuing with Blake's assessment:

**Is Blake receiving any benefits in their own right?** For example:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

(Children receiving these types of benefits in their own name are not eligible for Child Benefit)

I assume the answer is no for a 13-year-old in school, but I need to confirm this.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue checking Blake's eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Blake in any type of apprenticeship program?**

I'm asking because certain types of apprenticeships can affect eligibility, though this is more commonly relevant for older teenagers. Since Blake is only 13 and in regular school, I expect the answer is no, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any apprenticeship - they're just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Now let me move on to check some questions about **you as the claimant**:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if **any** of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**, OR
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, OR
3. You have pre-settled status and meet certain conditions (working, studying, job seeking, etc.), OR
4. You're a Crown servant living abroad

For most people, this is straightforward - do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK - I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, I need to check if you meet any of the special circumstances that would still allow you to claim Child Benefit.

**Do any of these situations apply to you?**

1. **Are you a Crown servant?** (working for the UK government abroad, such as diplomatic service, armed forces, etc.)

2. **Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme** and meet certain conditions?

3. **Are you living in certain qualifying countries** where you might still be eligible?

Please let me know if any of these apply to your situation, or if none of them do.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm just living abroad as a regular resident there.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, this means you do not meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## **Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Final Decision**

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Blake or Alex.

---

### **Summary for Each Child:**

**Blake (13 years old):** ❌ **Not Eligible**
- Blake meets all the qualifying child requirements (under 16, not working full-time, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship)
- However, your claim cannot proceed because you do not meet the UK residency requirements

**Alex (18 years old):** ❌ **Not Eligible**  
- While we didn't complete the full assessment regarding Alex's education status, the claim is ineligible regardless due to your residency situation

---

### **Why You're Not Eligible:**

**You do not meet the UK residency requirements.** To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying conditions), OR
- Be a Crown servant working abroad, OR
- Be living in certain qualifying countries

Since you're living abroad as a regular resident and none of the special circumstances apply to you, you cannot claim Child Benefit.

---

### **What This Means:**

Child Benefit is specifically for people who are resident in the UK or who meet very particular exceptions. Unfortunately, living abroad without one of those special circumstances means you cannot receive this benefit, even though your children would otherwise qualify.

---

### **Guidance:**

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you return to live in the UK), you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time. You can find more information at: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

I understand this isn't the outcome you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help clarify about this decision?

reasonings: Blake is 13 years old, not working full-time, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship, which means Blake meets all the qualifying child criteria. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and do not have settled/pre-settled status, are not a Crown servant, and are not in a qualifying country. Therefore, the claim for Blake is ineligible due to the claimant's residency status.

Alex is 18 years old (in the 16-19 age range). While we did not complete the full assessment for Alex's education status, the claim is ineligible regardless because the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad as a regular resident without settled/pre-settled status, is not a Crown servant, and is not in a qualifying country. Therefore, the claim for Alex is also ineligible due to the claimant's residency status.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is inquiring about Child Benefit for two children: Blake (13) and Alex (18). While Blake clearly meets the qualifying child criteria, and Alex's situation would require further education assessment, the fundamental issue is that the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad as a regular resident and does not meet any of the special circumstances (Crown servant, settled/pre-settled status, or living in qualifying countries) that would allow them to claim while outside the UK. Therefore, the claim is ineligible for both children due to the claimant's residency status.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
